metadata.description: Inode: Store file metadata. Do not store filenames. Hard link count manages the lifecycle. Python code example for managing hard links: ```python import os def create_hard_link(original_file, new_link_name): """ Create a hard link to an existing file. :param original_file: The path of the original file. :param new_link_name: The name of the new hard link. """ try: os.link(original_file, new_link_name) print(f"Hard link '{new_link_name}' created for file '{original_file}'.") except OSError as e: print(f"Error creating hard link: {e}") def remove_hard_link(linked_file): """ Remove a hard link to an existing file. :param linked_file: The path of the hard link to be removed. """ try: os.unlink(linked_file) print(f"Hard link '{linked_file}' removed.") except OSError as e: print(f"Error removing hard link: {e}") # Example usage original_path = 'example.txt' hard_link_path = 'example_hardlink.txt' create_hard_link(original_path, hard_link_path) # The file will still exist even if the original is deleted because of the hard link. os.remove(original_path) print(f"Original file '{original_path}' has been removed.") remove_hard_link(hard_link_path) ``` This Python script demonstrates creating and removing hard links, which can be used to manage the lifecycle of files through their hard link count.
